text:
I will provide you a transcript of a lecture and you will create a detailed notes for me. Make sure to cover all the topics. If needed fill in information for topics. 

[Transcript]
title: Youtube Explainer

text:
Create a 10-question multiple-choice quiz based on the following text to test my understanding. Include an answer key at the end. 

[Text]
title: Concept Quizzer

text: Create a structured study plan for [Topic] that fits within [Timeframe]. Include key milestones, recommended resources, and daily goals.
title: Study Planner

text:
Prioritize the following list of tasks based on urgency and impact using the Eisenhower Matrix. Explain why you categorized them that way. 

[Tasks]
title: Task Prioritizer

text:
Summarize the following meeting notes into key takeaways and actionable next steps. Assign owners to each action item if mentioned. 

[Notes]
title: Meeting Summarizer

text: Help me set SMART goals for [Project] with a final deadline of [Date]. Break them down into weekly targets.
title: SMART Goal Setter

text:
Generate 10 catchy headlines for the following article draft. Vary the styles between click-worthy, professional, and question-based. 

[Draft]
title: Headline Generator

text:
Simplify the following text to make it readable for a general audience without losing key information. Remove jargon where possible. 

[Text]
title: Simplifier

text: Write a blog post about [Topic] tailored for [Audience]. Ensure the tone is [Tone] and include a call to action at the end.
title: Blog Post Creator

text:
Draft a cold outreach email based on the following value proposition. Keep it under 150 words and focus on the benefit to the recipient. 

[Value Prop]
title: Cold Outreach Drafter

text:
Draft an empathetic response to the following customer complaint. Acknowledge their frustration and offer a clear solution. 

[Complaint]
title: Complaint Responder

text: Draft an email to [Name] requesting a meeting about [Topic]. Suggest [Number] available time slots and ask for their preference.
title: Meeting Requester

text:
Add detailed comments to the following code explaining what each function does. Use standard documentation style for the language. 

[Code]
title: Code Commenter

text:
Identify potential bugs in the following code snippet and suggest fixes. Highlight any security vulnerabilities or performance issues. 

[Code]
title: Bug Hunter

text:
Write unit tests for the following [Function] using the [Testing Framework] library. Ensure edge cases are covered. 

[Code]
title: Unit Test Writer

text: I am trying to make a decision about [Decision]. Please provide a comprehensive, unbiased list of the pros and cons, formatted as a clear comparison table.
title: Pro Cons

text: Explain the concept of [Concept] to me as if I am a complete beginner with no prior knowledge. Use an easy-to-understand, real-world analogy to make it click.
title: Explain Like I'm 5 (ELI5)

text: Rewrite the following text to sound completely [Target Tone]. Ensure the core message and facts remain identical, but change the vocabulary and sentence structure to fit the new vibe:
title: Tone Translation

text: Proofread the following text for grammar, punctuation, and clarity. Rewrite any awkward sentences so they flow perfectly, maintaining a [Tone] tone throughout:
title: Grammar & Flow Polish

text: Summarize this long email thread into exactly [Number] bullet points. Highlight any action items I specifically need to handle, and note the overarching tone of the sender:
title: TL;DR Summarizer

text: Draft a short, friendly follow-up email to [Name] regarding [Topic]. We last spoke on [Day/Date] and I am still waiting on their response. Keep the tone light and not overly pushy.
title: The "Checking In" Follow-up

text: Rewrite this draft email to sound highly professional, polite, but firm. The goal is to say "no" to [Request/Task] without burning bridges or sounding aggressive:
title: Polite Pushback

text: Write a Regular Expression (Regex) that matches [What to match]. It needs to work in [Language/Environment]. Please provide a few examples of what it will and will not match to verify.
title: Generate Regex

text: Refactor this [Language] code to be more clean, modular, and follow standard best practices. Maintain the exact original functionality, but add brief comments explaining your changes:
title: Refactor & Optimize

text: Explain the following [Language] code simply, line by line. Highlight any potential bugs, security vulnerabilities, or performance issues, and suggest how to fix them:
title: Code Explainer
